---
title: "Publications"
---

::: {.page-icon}
<img src="image/bookshop.svg" alt="" aria-hidden="true">
:::

# Welcome to my bookstore

Publishing papers is one of the things that occupies a large part of my world. I am fascinated by how people make decisions when technology begins to blur the line between human and machine. Much of my work explores how consumers and employees respond to AI-generated influencers, deepfakes, AI leaders, and AI supervision systems, with a particular interest in authenticity, trust, and wellbeing.

The publications below represent some of the questions that have kept me busy over the years; and in many cases, the questions I am still trying to answer.

:::{#tab-fund}

| No | Paper                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Status            | Journal Ranking             |
|----|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-----------------------------|
| 1  | **Cao,   T. T.**<sup>c</sup>, Prentice, C., Wang, Q., & Nguyen, S.   (2025). Financial self-discrepancy, consumer well-being, and luxury   preferences: The moderating effect of access-based luxury formats.   *Psychology & Marketing*, 43, 357–373.   [https://doi.org/10.1002/mar.70057](https://doi.org/10.1002/mar.70057)                                    | Published         | Q1,   IF 9.1, <br>  ABDC-A  |
| 2  | **Cao,   T. T.**, Prentice, C., Wang, Q., & Nguyen, H. S.<sup>c</sup>   (2026). Conflicted by faith, drawn to pleasure: How religious   self-discrepancy shapes product choices. *International Journal of Consumer   Studies*, 50(2), e70202. [https://doi.org/10.1111/ijcs.70202](https://doi.org/10.1111/ijcs.70202)                                            | Published         | Q1,   IF 7.7, <br>  ABDC-A  |
| 3  | **Cao,   T. T.**<sup>c</sup>, Prentice, C., Wang, Q., & Nguyen, H. S. (2025). Compensatory consumption: A review and research agenda using the   theory-context-characteristics-methodology framework. *International Journal   of Consumer Studies*, 49(5), e701112.   [https://doi.org/10.1111/ijcs.70112](https://doi.org/10.1111/ijcs.70112)                 | Published         | Q1,   IF 7.7, <br> ABDC-A   |
| 4  | **Cao,   T. T.**, Yunen, Z., & Tuan, N.<sup>c</sup> (2026). When   Generative Artificial Intelligence becomes a counsellor: How employee   evaluations shape engagement and organizational attractiveness. *Journal of   Enterprise Information Management*. [https://doi.org/10.1108/JEIM-02-2026-0238](https://doi.org/10.1108/JEIM-02-2026-0238) | Published          | Q1,   IF 7.6, <br> ABDC-A   |
| 5  | **Cao,   T. T.**, & Tuan, N.<sup>c</sup> (2026). Understanding consumer purchase intention toward Zero-Click GenAI-generated   recommendations: Evidence from structural equation modeling and machine   learning. *Journal of Internet Commerce*                                                                                                                | Accepted          | Q1,   IF 4.3, <br> ABDC-B   |
| 6  | **Cao,   T. T.**<sup>c</sup> (2025). Self-discrepancy and gambling   retention: A conceptual model of motivational and moral pathways. *Strategic   Business Research*, 1(1), 100001.   [https://doi.org/10.1016/j.sbr.2025.100001](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.sbr.2025.100001)                                                                                     | Published         | Peer-reviewed               |
| 7  | **Cao,   T. T.**, & Nguyen, S.<sup>c</sup> (2024). Navigating dual identities: How bicultural consumers leverage luxury consumption to cope with   perceived discrimination and enhance well-being. *Journal of Development and   Integration*, 85, 106–116.   [https://doi.org/10.61602/jdi.2025.85.12](https://doi.org/10.61602/jdi.2025.85.12)                | Published         | Peer-reviewed               |
| 8  | Ha,   N. M., & **Cao, T. T.**<sup>c</sup> (2019). Constructing athlete identification and the effectiveness of athlete endorsement on   customer's purchase intention. *Journal of Distribution Science*, 17(8).   [https://doi.org/10.15722/jds.17.8.201908.87](https://doi.org/10.15722/jds.17.8.201908.87)                                                    | Published         | Q3,   Peer-reviewed         |
| 9  | Mai,   N., **Cao, T.**<sup>c</sup>, & Yunen, Z. (2025). How hotels communicate sustainability goal revisions: The role of perceived   green-rinsing, influencer type, and sustainability solutions. *Journal of   Sustainable Tourism*                                                                                                                           | Third   revision  | Q1,   IF 9.9, <br> ABDC-A*  |
| 10 | Yunen,   Z., **Cao, T. T.**<sup>c</sup>, & Mai, N. (2025). The right   endorser for the right stay: How human and AI endorsers differ across privacy levels and service modes in accommodation booking intention. *International   Journal of Hospitality Management*                                                                                            | Second   revision | Q1,   IF 8.3, <br> ABDC-A*  |
| 11 | Mai,   N., **Cao, T.**, Yunen, Z.<sup>c</sup>, & Tingwen X. (2025).   Deepfake versus Virtual Influencers: How Influencer Diversity and Corporate   Social Responsibility Shape Consumer Ethicality and Engagement. *Journal of   Business Ethics*                                                                                                                 | First   revision  | Q1,   IF 8.7, <br> ABDC-A   |
| 12 | Zhang,   Y., Mai, N., **Cao, T. T.**<sup>c</sup> (2006) When Synthetic   Endorsers Are Morally Acceptable: Context-Dependent Consumer Responses to   AI-Generated Synthetic Endorsers. *Journal of Business Ethics*                                                                                                                                                | Under   Revision  | Q1,   IF 6.9, <br> ABDC-A*  |
| 13 |  **Cao, T. T.**, Mai, N., Tuan,   N.<sup>c</sup>, Malik, A., & Anjali, T. (2026). How employees   evaluate Artificial Intelligence-generated leaders: Perceived responsibility,   ethicality, and authenticity as pathways to compliance. *The Service   Industries Journal*                                                                                       | Second   revision | Q1,   IF 7.4, <br> ABDC-A   |
| 14 | **Cao,   T. T.**, Mai, N., & Yunen, Z.<sup>c</sup> (2026). Deepfake   versus human influencers in hospitality marketing: The roles of emotional   exhaustion, autonomy, and identity similarity in shaping booking intention.   *Journal of Hospitality and Tourism Management*                                                                                    | First   revision  | Q1,   IF 8.7, <br>  ABDC-A* |
| 15 | Nguyen,   M. , Cao, T. T., Nham, T.* ,Zhang, Y. (2026). AI supervision systems and   employee      voice: The mediating roles of perceived justice, credibility, and workplace   inclusion. *Personal Relationships*                                                                                                                                               | Submission        | Q1,   IF 2.2                |
| 16 | Nguyen,   M., **Cao, T. T.**, Yunen, Z.<sup>c</sup>, & Yang, B. (2026).   From Virtual Endorsers to Booking Intention: Understanding Consumer Responses   to AI-Generated Lodging Advertising. *Tourism Management*                                                                                                                                                | Submission        |  Q1, IF 9.9,<br> ABDC-A     |
| 17 | **Cao,   T. T.**, Mai, N., & Yunen, Z.<sup>c</sup> (2026). Deepfake   versus human influencers in hospitality marketing: The roles of emotional   exhaustion, autonomy, and identity similarity in shaping booking intention.   *Journal of Hospitality and Tourism Management*                                                                                    | Submission        | Q1,   IF 8.7, <br>  ABDC-A* |
| 18 | Tuan,   N., & **Cao, T. T.**<sup>c</sup>, Manlio, D, G., Francesco,   C., & Muath, A, T.  (2026). Working   with GenAI: How human-centered mindset and GenAI understanding drive employee   autonomy and competence. *Journal of Enterprise Information Management*                                                                                                | Submission        | Q1,   IF 7.6, <br> ABDC-A   |
| 19 | Mai,   N., Brooks, Z., Tazeem, W.<sup>c</sup>, & **Cao, T. T.**   (2026). Trust in AI supervision system and employee proactivity.   *Technological Forecasting and Social Change*                                                                                                                                                                                 | First   revision  | Q1,   IF 13.3, <br> ABDC-A  |
| 20 | Huong,   P., Thinh, D., & **Cao, T. T.**<sup>c</sup> (2026). Revaluing   waste: The roles of true-to-ideal authenticity, actual-ought discrepancy, and   user-generated content in willingness to pay for upcycled food.   *Agribusiness*.                                                                                                                         | Submission        | Q1,   IF 2.0                |
| 21 | **Cao,   T. T.**, Ashish, M., & Mai, N. <sup>c</sup> (2026). *Employee   Evaluations of AI-generated Leaders: A Mixed-Methods Analysis of Uncanniness,   Transparency, and Employee Outcomes.* In Proceedings of the 21st International Forum on Knowledge Asset Dynamics (IFKAD 2026)                                                                           | Presented         | 1-3   July, <br> Hungary    |

:::
